title: Victims Guidance (January 2025 v2.0)
body: "The guidance provides information on the Parole Board’s duties towards victims under the Code of Practice for Victims of Crime which came into force on 1 April 2021 and changes introduced since the guidance was last published in February 2020. Topics covered include:   Relevant legal frameworks The Probation Victim Contact Scheme Victim Personal Statements Reading out Victim Personal Statements at parole oral hearings Observing a parole oral hearing Summary decisions Routes of challenge   Following the publication of the guidance two position statements related to victim observers were issued to take account of feedback:   Statement one provides clarity about when a victim may need to make a new observer application Statement two sets out a proposed change in our position on victims taking notes when observing an oral hearing.   The Parole Board has also published the following documents:  Victims and Family and Friends of Victims – parole hearings: Information Leaflet  Victim Liaison Officers – aide memoire on parole  More detailed information about considering victim related licence conditions can be found in the Guidance on Licence Conditions.  Other relevant guidance that may be helpful:  The Prisoners (Disclosure of Information About Victims) Act guidance  Parole Board member guidance on Restorative Justice  Observer guidance - private hearings  More information for victims can be read here: Our Commitment to Victims of Crime "
